{"id":"project-LICHEN-6so1","title":"LichenFrame.to_bytes does not include signature in serialization","description":"File: python/src/lichen/link/frame.py:127-146, link_layer.py:254-256

The frame format includes a signature_present flag (bit 5 of LLSec), but the signature is embedded in the payload, not as a separate field. LichenFrame has no signature field.

In link_layer.py:254-256:
    signed_payload = payload + signature
    frame = LichenFrame(..., payload=signed_payload, signature_present=True)

This means:
1. Frame.payload contains application data + signature concatenated
2. Frame.signature_present is True but there's no Frame.signature field
3. Receiver must know to strip signature from end of payload

The serialization/deserialization is asymmetric. to_bytes() serializes payload (which may contain signature), but from_bytes() doesn't extract the signature. This is handled in link_layer.receive() but could be confusing.

Consider: Add optional signature field to LichenFrame, or document this design clearly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:28.693658Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:28.693693Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-6so1","title":"LichenFrame.to_bytes does not include signature in serialization","description":"File: python/src/lichen/link/frame.py:127-146, link_layer.py:254-256

The frame format includes a signature_present flag (bit 5 of LLSec), but the signature is embedded in the payload, not as a separate field. LichenFrame has no signature field.

In link_layer.py:254-256:
    signed_payload = payload + signature
    frame = LichenFrame(..., payload=signed_payload, signature_present=True)

This means:
1. Frame.payload contains application data + signature concatenated
2. Frame.signature_present is True but there's no Frame.signature field
3. Receiver must know to strip signature from end of payload

The serialization/deserialization is asymmetric. to_bytes() serializes payload (which may contain signature), but from_bytes() doesn't extract the signature. This is handled in link_layer.receive() but could be confusing.

Consider: Add optional signature field to LichenFrame, or document this design clearly.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:28.693658Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T23:32:38.434599627Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T23:32:38.434599627Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T23:37:38.434599627Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T23:32:38.434599627Z"}

new_value: Updated LichenFrame docs for signature-in-mic design; added missing ReceiveError enum and fixed receive() docstring + merge conflict; tests and ruff lint now pass; aligns with Rust implementation
